title: Multiple attack modes
principles: Each confirmed attack mode must be evaluated independently before producing a whole-weapon conclusion.
Mode records, internal states, auxiliary components, and fallback entries must remain distinct record types and must not be treated as interchangeable.
Statistics, damage components, timing, costs, and mechanics from separate modes must not be combined into one synthetic attack.
Mode distinction must be evaluated from explicit differences in input, timing, delivery, components, cost, accessibility, or activation conditions rather than from labels or ordering alone.
Statistical similarity does not prove that two records represent the same practical mode when their state, activation, or provenance differs.
Whole-weapon conclusions may summarize several confirmed modes, but every claim must remain traceable to the mode that supports it.
Source designation, player accessibility, and analytical relevance must remain separate properties.

single_confirmed_mode: The supplied records establish one player-facing or mechanically relevant attack mode.
Other records, if present, do not qualify as independent modes.
multiple_distinct_modes: Two or more confirmed modes differ in at least one supplied mechanical or accessibility dimension.
Each mode must preserve its own statistics, rhythm, delivery, costs, and conclusions.
equivalent_or_duplicate_records: Several records appear to represent the same mode without a supported mechanical distinction.
Treat them as equivalent only when provenance, activation, and component evidence also support equivalence.
conditional_mode: The record represents a distinct attack mode available only under a supplied condition or state.
Preserve its activation requirement and do not describe it as permanently available.
internal_state: The record represents a phase or state of another mode rather than an independently selected attack mode.
Associate it with its parent mode when that relationship is supplied.
auxiliary_component_record: The record describes a component, subattack, or implementation detail rather than a complete player-facing mode.
Do not promote it to an independent mode.
ambiguous_modes: The supplied records indicate possible mode variation but do not establish record type, accessibility, or mechanical distinction with sufficient confidence.
Preserve the records separately without assigning equivalence or dominance.

source_primary_designation: Preserve an explicit primary-mode designation as source metadata.
Do not treat that designation alone as proof that the mode defines the weapon's primary practical job.
state_name_present: Treat state_name as state evidence rather than mode evidence unless independent input, activation, timing, or delivery differences establish a distinct mode.
duplicate_or_fallback_provenance: Treat duplicated, inherited, fallback, or generated records as non-distinct unless additional evidence establishes separate mechanical relevance.
mode_specific_analysis: When the requested analysis targets one mode, restrict conclusions and improvement selection to that mode and its associated states or components.
shared_resource_or_component: Preserve shared magazine, reserve, component, or activation relationships without merging the modes' independent statistics.

exceptions: Do not merge damage, critical, status, timing, ammunition, or delivery values across modes.
Do not assume that an unlabeled record is an alternate fire.
Do not assume that every normalized record is player-selectable or independently activated.
Do not identify a dominant mode from ordering, naming, source-primary designation, or higher damage alone.
Do not declare two records equivalent from matching statistics alone.
Do not let one mode overwrite another mode's evidence or conclusions.
